subtopics.title: Atomic and Nuclear Physics
common_misconceptions: All radiation is harmful (background radiation is natural)
Nuclear fission and fusion are the same
Half-life means half the atoms die
subtopics.teaching_note: Key equations: Alpha (α): ⁴₂He nucleus, stopped by paper. Beta (β): electron (⁰₋₁e), stopped by aluminium. Gamma (γ): electromagnetic, stopped by lead. Half-life: time for half of radioactive atoms to decay. N = N₀(1/2)^(t/T½). Activity A = λN. Fission: heavy nucleus splits (uranium-235 + neutron → smaller nuclei + energy). Fusion: light nuclei combine (H + H → He + energy, occurs in stars). Applications: nuclear power, medical imaging, radiocarbon dating, sterilization. Nigerian context: Nigeria plans nuclear power with Rosatom (Russia). A.E. Ekukinam Nuclear Research Centre at UniPort.

subtopics.title: Semiconductors and Electronic Devices
common_misconceptions: Semiconductors are partial conductors (conductivity can be controlled)
Transistors only amplify (also used as switches)
Logic gates are only theoretical (basis of all digital devices)
subtopics.teaching_note: Semiconductors: Si, Ge. Doping: adding impurities. N-type: excess electrons (P, As dopants). P-type: holes (B, Al dopants). PN junction = diode. Forward bias: conducts. Reverse bias: blocks. Transistor: NPN or PNP. Amplification: small input controls larger output. Logic gates: AND (all inputs 1 → output 1), OR (any input 1 → output 1), NOT (inverts), NAND, NOR. Truth tables. Applications: computers, phones, calculators, radios. Nigerian context: electronics market at Alaba International, computer village Ikeja.
